rationale: Both notes indicate strong conceptual overlap: the citing paper’s core contribution is estimating asymmetric switching costs in a dynamic supermarket game, while the cited paper develops and calibrates a dynamic oligopoly model of switching costs and competition under Markov perfect equilibrium. That makes the cited work a plausible theory/mechanism source rather than an incidental mention. Given this relevance (despite no in-text snippet), it should be retained for full relation judgment.
